author: Admin
content:
Today should be another day full of awesomeness and joy brought about by everything and everyone I have been interacting with. This happened both at and from work. It was a day that started with a lot of anxiety as my boss usually comes to work early when there are crises to deal with, and I knew I was part of it. Miss Bonobo brought about some level of anxiety too. As a result, I must plus my minus, but the day ended as a good one though. The fatigue had carried me away, but I was grateful...........

Apparently, I hadn’t realized it was my good friend’s birthday and immediately I left the office, I had to grab our favourite beer, the Mighty Guinness. He is a nice character and company, and three Guinness rounds later, kila mtu akaenda kwake................
Such a day.

title: The Art of Slow Living in Nairobi
subtitle: Finding peace amidst the rhythmic drums of the city.
author: Admin
content:
Nairobi is a city that never truly sleeps. From the early morning matatu horns to the distant hum of nightlife, the energy is infectious. but sometimes, you need to step back.

I sat by the window today, watching the jacarandas bloom. It was a moment of pure, unadulterated presence.
pullQuote: In the rush of the city, the most revolutionary act is to simply be still.
sceneCard: The air smelled of wet earth and roasted maize. A single matatu sped past, its neon lights blurring into a streak of purple against the gray sky.
closingBox: Slow down. Not because the world is too fast, but because your soul deserves to catch up.

title: The monster in the City
subtitle: The subttle life of Nairobi
author: Admin
content: Simply sideloading the Play Store APK may not be enough on Sunmi devices. The Play Store also requires Google Play Services (GMS) to be active — which is what you're trying to fix. If GMS is disabled/broken, the Play Store won't work even after installing the APK.
pullQuote:
Download the APK from APKMirror on your phone/PC
Transfer to the Sunmi V3 via USB or download directly on the device
Go to Settings → Security → enable Install from Unknown Sources
Tap the APK file to install
sceneCard: ou may need to contact Sunmi support to request GMS activation for your specific device, as some Sunmi V3 versions include Google Mobile Services (GMS) while others are non-GMS builds for more customized setups OrderPin — and the non-GMS version cannot run Play Store regardless of what APK you install.

title: Kenya is a country in East Africa with coastline on the Indian Ocean.
author: Admin
content: Kenya is a country in East Africa with coastline on the Indian Ocean. It encompasses savannah, lakelands, the dramatic Great Rift Valley and mountain highlands. It's also home to wildlife like lions, elephants and rhinos. From Nairobi, the capital, safaris visit the Maasai Mara Reserve, known for its annual wildebeest migrations, and Amboseli National Park, offering views of Tanzania's 5,895m Mt. Kilimanjar
closingBox: Kenya is a country in East Africa with coastline on the Indian Ocean.
pullQuote: Kenya is a country in East Africa with coastline on the Indian Ocean.
subtitle: Kenya is a country in East Africa with coastline on the Indian Ocean.

title: How AI Is Quietly Transforming the Way We Work
subtitle: Let's be real — AI isn't coming for your job
author: Admin
content:
Let's be real — AI isn't coming for your job. It's already sitting next to you at your desk, and honestly, it's not bad company.
For business professionals, AI tools are becoming as routine as email. From drafting reports and summarizing meeting notes to analyzing data in seconds, the productivity gains are hard to ignore. Tools like Claude, ChatGPT, and Copilot are saving teams hours every week — time that can go toward the work that actually requires a human touch.
But here's the thing: the professionals getting the most out of AI aren't the ones who know the most about technology. They're the ones asking the best questions. AI is only as useful as the prompts you give it.
So where do you start? Pick one repetitive task you do every week and try handing it off to an AI tool. Maybe it's writing first drafts, sorting through emails, or summarizing research. Start small, build confidence, and scale up.
The future of work isn't AI versus humans — it's humans who use AI versus those who don't. And the gap between the two is widening faster than most people realize. The best time to start experimenting was yesterday. The second best time? Right now.
pullQuote: But here's the thing: the professionals getting the most out of AI aren't the ones who know the most about technology. They're the ones asking the best questions. AI is only as useful as the prompts you give it.
sceneCard: The future of work isn't AI versus humans — it's humans who use AI versus those who don't.
closingBox: he gap between the two is widening faster than most people realize. The best time to start experimenting was yesterday. The second best time? Right now.

title: Why Every Business Needs a Side Strategy (Not Just a Side Hustle)
subtitle: There's a big difference between a side hustle and a side strategy —
author: Admin
content:
There's a big difference between a side hustle and a side strategy — and the distinction might change how you think about growing your business.
A side hustle is something you do for extra income. A side strategy is something you build deliberately to open new doors, test new markets, or future-proof your main venture. Think of it as a calculated experiment rather than just a weekend project.
Some of the most successful business pivots in history started as side strategies. Amazon started as an online bookstore before it became everything. Slack was originally a gaming company's internal communication tool. Netflix mailed DVDs before it disrupted television.
For entrepreneurs and business professionals, the question isn't whether you have time for a side strategy — it's whether you can afford not to have one. Markets shift. Industries evolve. The businesses that survive are the ones that were already testing what's next.
Start by asking: what adjacent opportunity could we explore without compromising our core business? A small bet today could become your biggest revenue stream in five years. Smart businesses don't wait for disruption — they engineer it themselves.
pullQuote: There's a big difference between a side hustle and a side strategy — and the distinction might change how you think about growing your business.
sceneCard: what adjacent opportunity could we explore without compromising our core business? A small bet today could become your biggest revenue stream in five years. Smart businesses don't wait for disruption — they engineer it themselves.
closingBox: For entrepreneurs and business professionals, the question isn't whether you have time for a side strategy — it's whether you can afford not to have one. Markets shift. Industries evolve. The businesses that survive are the ones that were already testing what's next.

title: Here's something most high performers don't want to hear:
subtitle: Here's something most high performers don't want to hear:
author: Admin
content:
Here's something most high performers don't want to hear: skipping lunch isn't a productivity hack. It's a slow leak in your performance.
Research consistently shows that breaks — especially midday ones — improve focus, decision-making, and creativity. Your brain isn't a machine you can run at full capacity for eight hours straight. It needs downtime to consolidate information, reset attention, and recharge.
Yet in many corporate cultures, eating at your desk while answering emails has become a badge of honor. We've confused busyness with effectiveness, and we're paying for it in burnout, poor decisions, and chronic stress.
The fix doesn't have to be complicated. Step away from your screen for 20–30 minutes. Eat something nourishing. Take a short walk. Have an actual conversation that isn't about deliverables. These aren't luxuries — they're inputs for sustainable performance.
The most effective leaders understand that energy management is just as important as time management. You can schedule every hour of your day perfectly, but if you're running on empty by 2 PM, none of it matters. Protect your lunch break like it's a meeting with your most important client. Because in a way, it is.
pullQuote: The fix doesn't have to be complicated. Step away from your screen for 20–30 minutes.
sceneCard: The fix doesn't have to be complicated. Step away from your screen for 20–30 minutes.
closingBox: The most effective leaders understand that energy management is just as important as time management.

title: Arsenal Big game at Manchester City
subtitle: Arsenal have to win this game or TITLE dream DWINDLES
author: Admin
content: Arsenal travel to the Etihad today in what could be the defining moment of their entire season. Sitting top of the Premier League with 70 points, the Gunners SHOULD be in control — but they're not. One win in five across all competitions, a shock loss to Bournemouth last weekend, and City are suddenly breathing down their necks. What was a 97% title probability has already crumbled. Mikel Arteta's side cannot afford another slip. This isn't just three points — this is Arsenal's chance to silence the doubters and finally end that 22-year wait. Win. Or watch it all unravel.
pullQuote: This isn't just three points — this is Arsenal's chance to silence the doubters and finally end that 22-year wait. Win. Or watch it all unravel
sceneCard:
"
"
This isn't just three points —
this is Arsenal's chance to
silence the doubters.
Win. Or watch it all unravel.
ARSENAL vs MAN CITY · APRIL 19, 2026
closingBox: Twenty-two years of hurt, heartbreak, and hope — today, at the Etihad, Arsenal fans demand the win that finally changes everything.

title: Another gut punch at the Etihad. Again. 😤
author: Admin
content:
Honestly, what is there even left to say? We go to Manchester City, show genuine quality, take the lead — and then somehow find a way to hand it back. It's the same story, different chapter. Defensively, we looked shaky the moment City turned up the pressure, and it cost us dearly.
The title race is slipping through our fingers at the worst possible time. With Bournemouth already getting a result against us earlier this month, and now this — you have to wonder if this squad has the mentality to actually go all the way. Talent? Absolutely. Bottle? That's the question nobody wants to answer.
Arteta will talk about "processes" and "belief," and fair enough — but the table doesn't lie. Points dropped in big moments define seasons, and we keep failing those tests.
Five games left. Still mathematically in it. But right now, it feels more like damage limitation than a title charge. The frustrating thing is we're good enough — we just don't seem to know it when it matters most.

title: Before the Projects, There Were the People
subtitle: I Didn't Plan This Path — But I'm Grateful For It
author: Admin
content:
I didn't start at Solutech as a Project Manager. I started at the front line — customer service — and that distinction matters more than most people realise.
Before I was managing projects, I was managing people's frustrations, their expectations, their urgency. Every call, every ticket, every follow-up taught me something that no classroom could. I learned how clients think, what they actually need versus what they say they need, and how to stay composed when things go sideways.
That foundation turned out to be everything when the opportunity to transition into project management came. I wasn't starting from scratch — I was building on something real. The empathy, the communication, the understanding of how delivery failures feel on the receiving end — all of it came with me.
The transition wasn't without its challenges. There was a steep learning curve, new responsibilities, and moments of self-doubt that I won't pretend didn't exist. But I kept showing up.
Since December 2024, I look back at that customer service desk not with embarrassment, but with gratitude. It was the best training ground I never signed up for.
pullQuote: I didn't start at Solutech as a Project Manager. I started at the front line — customer service — and that distinction matters more than most people realise.
sceneCard: Sixteen months later, I look back at that customer service desk not with embarrassment, but with gratitude. It was the best training ground I never signed up for.
closingBox: Sometimes your starting point is quietly preparing you for where you're actually going.

title: check out
subtitle: nvestigate/Verify: "I need to check out those facts before I believe them"
author: Admin
content:
e Examples

    Investigate/Verify: "I need to check out those facts before I believe them".
    Examine/Look at: "Check out the view from this window!".
    Leave (Hotel/Hospital): "We have to check out by 11:00 AM".
    Pay for goods: "She is checking out at the register now".
    Borrow: "I checked out three books from the library".
    Attraction (Slang): "Check out that guy in the red shirt".
